intro: Hutera johnstonii, also known as Heteranthus johnstonii, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of New Caledonia, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful for wound healing and the treatment of various skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Hutera johnstonii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Hutera johnstonii is often used in traditional medicine and is typically prepared as a decoction, infusion, or ointment to harness its therapeutic benefits.
